name: Black Bean Burritos
recipeIngredient: 5 tablespoons Olive Oil or Vegetable Oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the dry beans if using: Rinse and drain canned beans or follow steps below to cook dried beans.
2. In a 4-1/2-quart Dutch oven, heat oil over medium heat. Add onion, garlic, peppers; stir until onions become translucent.
3. Stir in chili powder and cumin; cook 1 minute more.
4. If using dry beans, add beans and enough water to cover by 2 inches. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 2 minutes. Remove from heat, cover, and let stand 1 hour (or soak overnight).
5. Drain beans, return them to pot with remaining water/broth; bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer covered until tender, about 1-1/2 hours.
6. Stir in tomatoes, lemon slice, oregano, salt (omit if using canned beans), and hot pepper sauce (if desired). Reduce heat, cover, and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes or until thick.
7. Remove lemon slices. In a blender or food processor, blend one-third of the mixture until smooth; set aside.
8. Repeat with remaining mixture. Return to pot and stir through.
9. Wrap tortillas in foil and warm in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for about 10 minutes.
10. Place about 1/2 cup bean mixture on each tortilla, fold edges to form packets. Serve with salsa and guacamole if desired; top with chopped tomato and cilantro.
